titleStmt.title: SII 10.389: original edition by J. Ramayya Pantulu
No. 389. (A.R. No. 104 of 1917.) ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE SHRINE OF THE GODDESS IN THE TEMPLE OF MALLIKĀRJUNA, MANDAPĀḌU, GUNTUR TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT. Date missing.

summary.p: The beginning is lost. The first nine lines of the remaining portion refer to a certain Prōla, son of Vidda-Nāyaka and Vennama. The remaining lines contain details of lands granted to certain temples and persons. (The beginning is lost.)

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
sūramāṁbikaku mikkili punyuṁḍu
dāra(kī)kīrtti lakṣmīlalanāsamētu
ṁḍu samīhitacittuṁḍu vuṭṭe goṁkka
bhūpālaku niṣṭabhrityuṁḍu sabhāma
ṇi maṁddaḍikōṭaṁ ḍoppagānu . 1 . vinu
takīrttiyutuṁḍu viddanāyakunaku dhara
ṇi vennamaku mudaṁbutōḍabrōlaṁ ḍu daya
mayyeṁ budhapanidhānuṁḍu vibhavaparuṁḍu
mu veluṁgu cuṁḍa . 2 . ganēsvarunaku kha 1
[Ū]riyuttaramu dēvatanivēdyānaku na 10
lu bhīmakālajñulamaṁḍabhaṭla kha 5
rēbhaṭla kha 2 doṇayabhaṭla kha 2 Amara bhaṭla kha
poyigāramu kaṁddāmara Eṟamōju kha
svadattaṁ paradattaṁ vā yō harēta vasuṁdha rāṁ ṣa
ṣṭi varuṣasahasrāṇi viṣṭāyāṁ jāyatē
krimi .

Note:—The letters ḍippaliyaśāsamaṁḍu Are Interpolated between lines 8 And 9 but they cannot be read Along with the rest Of the Inscription so As to make sense. The verses Are complete without these words.
Digital edition of SII 10.389 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
